description: Ruby on Rails web application with PostgreSQL database, user authentication with Devise, user registration with personal data including name email and social security number, employee management system with salary and benefits data, file upload for pay stubs, admin panel with role-based authorization, password reset via email token, internal messaging system, retirement fund management

vulnerabilities.name: SQL Injection
vulnerabilities.description: SQL injection in search and data retrieval using string interpolation in queries

vulnerabilities.name: Mass Assignment
vulnerabilities.description: Unprotected attributes allow role escalation via mass assignment

vulnerabilities.name: Authentication Weakness
vulnerabilities.description: Weak password policy, no account lockout, predictable password reset tokens

vulnerabilities.name: Cross-Site Scripting
vulnerabilities.description: Stored XSS in internal messages and employee profile fields

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Direct Object Reference
vulnerabilities.description: IDOR allowing access to other employees' salary and benefits data

vulnerabilities.name: Sensitive Data Exposure (SSN/Salary)
vulnerabilities.description: SSN and salary data stored in plaintext, exposed via unprotected endpoints

vulnerabilities.name: Missing Access Control
vulnerabilities.description: Admin functionality accessible to regular users

vulnerabilities.name: CSRF
vulnerabilities.description: CSRF protection disabled or improperly implemented

vulnerabilities.name: Session Management Weakness
vulnerabilities.description: Session not invalidated on logout, no session timeout

vulnerabilities.name: File Upload Vulnerability
vulnerabilities.description: Unrestricted file upload for pay stubs without content validation

vulnerabilities.name: Information Leakage
vulnerabilities.description: Detailed error pages in production, exposed database schema information
